title: MEDICAL CLINIC - 211info
text: OUTSIDE IN MEDICAL CLINIC Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF A primary care clinic that provides diagnosis and treatment for acute and chronic medical care, such as infections and diabetes. Services also include general physical exams, pregnancy testing, STI testing, and abscess and other common illness treatments. Limited urgent care, mental, and behavioral health services available. 1132 SW 13th Avenue Portland, OR 97205 Eligibility: All ages and genders, with a focus on serving youth, homeless, and LQBTQI individuals Hours: Monday/Thursday/Friday 8:30am-5pm, Tuesday-Wednesday 8:30am-7pm. Closed until 10am on the second Tuesday of the month. Email (503) 535-3860 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Scheduling (503) 535-3860 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Walk in or call to schedule an appointment. Walk-in clients may have to wait long periods. Same-day appointments are limited. FEES: None for individuals under age 21. Sliding scale fees are available to those under 200% of the federal poverty level. No one is turned away for inability to pay. Accepts Medicare, OHP Open Card, HealthShare (CareOregon, OHSU, Providence), Trillium, and most private insurance. Insurance will be billed, if applicable. DOCUMENTS: Insurance information, if applicable. Proof of income if applying for sliding scale fees. LANGUAGES: English, Spanish, interpretation by arrangement AREA SERVED: Clackamas, Multnomah, and Washington Counties SITE HOURS: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm Services: Alternative Medicine Community Clinics General Physical Examinations HIV Testing Sexually Transmitted Infection Screening Other Services Offered At This Location: HEALTH SCREENING/DIAGNOSTIC SERVICES - SCREENWISE-ENROLLING HOUSING/SHELTER - OUTSIDE IN DAY PROGRAM HOUSING/SHELTER - YOUTH HOUSING PROGRAM HUMAN REPRODUCTION - OREGON CONTRACEPTIVE CARE LEGAL SERVICES - ID PROJECT SPECIALIZED TREATMENT AND PREVENTION - TRANS CLINIC If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service.
